exemplar.title: How does the extension of an elastic slingshot affect the displacement of an object due to the transfer of kinetic energy from the projectile?

detailedExemplarData.title: How does the extension of an elastic slingshot affect the displacement of an object due to the transfer of kinetic energy from the projectile?

subject.title: Physics
subject.description:
**IB Physics** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **space, time and motion; the particulate nature of matter; wave behaviour; fields; and nuclear and quantum physics**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, data analysis, and scientific investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice questions and data-based questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 1 hour 30 minutes | 30% |
| **Paper 2** | Short-answer and extended-response questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 2 hours 15 minutes | 50% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Individual scientific investigation | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Data tables and graphs are clearly labeled with units and uncertainties, including sample calculations
Processing of means, percent uncertainties, gradient and efficiency calculations is accurate
Presentation (titles, axis labels, bestâfit/minâmax lines) is clear and precise
weaknesses: Uncertainty in the independent variable (extension) is never propagated or shown in the main graph
Error bars are absent from key figures, reducing visual communication of precision
Efficiency calculations lack propagated uncertainties and discussion of significance
Pearson R value reported without commentary on statistical confidence
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two strands (recording/processing and processing quality) meet the top band (5â6), but consideration of uncertainties sits solidly in the middle band (3â4) with significant omissions in propagation and visualisation. Taken together, the work is more than limited but falls short of fully appropriate treatment of uncertainties, warranting a score of 3.

strengths: Focused research question embedded in a clear, specific elastic-slingshot context
Background theory (Hookeâs law and energy conservation) explicitly linked to apparatus
Detailed materials list, variable table and annotated photos support reproducibility
Methodological choices (number of levels/trials, k-value derivation, safety trials) are justified and relevant
weaknesses: Minor ambiguity in phrasing of the research question (âtransfer of kinetic energy from the projectileâ could be more precise)
No discussion of frictional or air-resistance effects to justify energy-loss assumptions
Step-by-step method omits specification of an alignment tool or tolerance for the aiming line
Key derivation of the spring constant relegated to appendix rather than main text
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands fall clearly into the top band (5â6): the question is specific, the methodology reproducible, and methodological considerations are well explained. Minor imprecisions (ambiguity in phrasing, lack of friction discussion, aiming specification) prevent the full jump to 6, so a score of 5 accurately reflects the strong but not perfect performance.

strengths: Conclusion directly references numerical trend, gradient and correlation coefficient to confirm the hypothesis
Statement is consistent with processed data and supported by uncertainty discussion
Comparison to accepted scientific context (Hookeâs law, energy conservation) is relevant and well justified
weaknesses: Does not compare the empirical gradient quantitatively to the theoretical value predicted by energy equations
Some discussion of limitations from losses is implied but could be more explicitly tied to the final statement
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Both strands are clearly in the high band (5â6) but the omission of a quantitative comparison between empirical and theoretical gradients constitutes a non-trivial gap. A score of 4 reflects a well-justified conclusion that is consistent with analysis yet not fully exhaustive.

strengths: Specific methodological weaknesses are identified (angle deviation, mis-reading of extension, energy losses) and relative impacts are explained
Realistic and relevant improvements (mechanical launcher, more trials, literature comparison) are proposed and linked to error reduction
weaknesses: Evaluation lacks quantitative linkage of uncertainty ranges to the slopeâs confidence interval
Formatting issues (duplicated header in the systematic error table) detract slightly from clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Both strands meet the descriptor for high performance (5â6) by explaining impacts and suggesting improvements, but the lack of quantitative connection of uncertainties to the final result and minor presentation flaws prevent top-band credit. A score of 4 reflects strong evaluation with room for deeper quantitative analysis.

strandSelections.reasoning: The student formulates a focused research question and embeds it within a clearly-defined practical context â a specific elastic slingshot propelling a box on a wooden ramp.  Background theory (Hookeâs law, conservation of energy) is explicitly connected to the chosen apparatus and predicted relationship, demonstrating that the context is both specific and appropriate.  Minor imprecision in the wording of the question (âtransfer of kinetic energy from the projectileâ) does not obscure the contextual clarity, so a mark of 5 in the top band is warranted.

Instrument uncertainties are quoted and propagated for mean displacement and for the gradient; percent uncertainties are calculated and discussed.  However, uncertainty in the independent variable (extension) is not propagated, error bars are absent from the main graph, and final efficiencies lack uncertainty treatment.  These omissions place the work solidly in the 3-4 band; the treatment is more than limited but not fully thorough, so 4 marks are given.

A full variable table explains relevance of each control and how it is managed; methodological choices (five levels, five trials, derivation of k-value, preliminary safety trials) are justified and linked to answering the question.  The student explains why the projectile type was altered and how the ramp surface and mass influence results, demonstrating awareness of data sufficiency and relevance.  Explanations are present but not exhaustive, so 5 marks in the top band are appropriate.

Specific weaknesses (angle deviation, extension mis-reading, unknown proportionality limit, energy losses) are identified and the student explains qualitatively how each could affect displacement values or validity, demonstrating understanding of relative impact.  This meets the descriptor for the highest band; 5 marks are assigned.

Materials lists, annotated photographs, five clearly-spaced extension values and five repeated trials per level allow another investigator to replicate the work with minimal ambiguity.  The procedure specifies how to align, measure and reset the box, and gives measurement resolutions for every instrument.  While the aiming method could be described in more detail, overall the description is sufficiently complete for reproduction; therefore a high-band placement with 5 marks is suitable.

Means, ranges, percent uncertainties, gradient determination and efficiency calculations are all carried out correctly and with appropriate significant figures.  No substantial computational errors or inconsistencies are evident, placing the processing in the highest band at 5 marks.

For every weakness a realistic and relevant improvement is proposed (mechanical launcher, more trials, measuring proportionality limit, literature comparison).  Explanations link each suggestion to reduction of error or increase in credibility, satisfying the criteria for the top band; hence 5 marks are appropriate.

Raw and processed data tables are clearly labelled with units and uncertainties; graphs include titles, axis labels and best-fit as well as min/max lines.  Calculations are shown and sample working is provided.  Presentation is both clear and precise, meriting placement in the highest band with 5 marks.

Results are interpreted in light of Hookeâs law, elastic potential energy and conservation of energy, showing how the empirical gradient relates to theoretical expectations.  The comparison to accepted physics is relevant and well-justified, warranting 5 marks in the top band.

The conclusion directly references the numerical trend, gradient and correlation coefficient, explicitly confirming the hypothesis and quantifying the relationship.  It is fully consistent with the processed data and supported by uncertainty discussion, justifying a high-band award of 5 marks.

annotations.comment: Processed data table effectively communicates s_avg and percent uncertainties, but lacks extension uncertainty column.

The research question wording âtransfer of kinetic energy from the projectileâ is ambiguous. Consider specifying the transfer pathway and energy forms to improve clarity and ensure the question is reproducible.
Background section expertly links Hookeâs law and energy conservation to the apparatus, establishing a specific context for the investigation.
The background omits any mention of frictional or airâresistance effects. Including a brief discussion would justify assumptions in energy conversion.
Hypothesis statement is thorough but assumes perfect proportionality. Clarify any underlying assumptions or limitations due to losses.
Variable table clearly identifies independent and controlled variables, enhancing reproducibility.
The tableâs heading âDependant Variableâ has a typo; correct to âDependentâ and ensure consistency.
Materials list is exhaustive and includes measurement resolutions, supporting clarity and reproducibility.
K constant derivation is relegated to the appendix; summarizing key procedure and result in the main text would improve coherence.
Figure 1 lacks error bars or uncertainty region. Adding error bars would enhance precision and visual communication.
Step 3 in the method describes using an âaiming lineâ but does not specify tolerance or device for angle control. Detail the alignment tool or angle measurement.
Figure 2 setup photograph is clear, but lacks scale markers. Including labeled dimensions would improve methodological clarity.
Uncertainty propagation did not include the independent variableâs uncertainty. Propagate Â±0.001 m extension uncertainty into gradient analysis.
Raw data table caption omits uncertainty for each reading. Specify Â±0.001 m for each displacement entry to maintain clarity.
Figure 3 is wellâconstructed but omits error bars. Represent uncertainties visually to enhance precision.
The Pearson R value (0.986) is reported without discussion of statistical significance or confidence. Add commentary on reliability.
Uncertainty of gradient calculation is sound, but describe method rationale (why halfârange approximates standard dev.)
Conclusion restates trends well but omits comparison of empirical gradient to theoretical value from energy equations. Include this.
Uncertainty discussion in evaluation describes ranges but does not link these quantitatively to the slopeâs confidence interval.
Efficiency analysis lacks uncertainty propagation for work and energy values. Include error bounds for Î·.
In the systematic error table, a duplicated header row appears (âSource of Errorâ, âImpact on Resultsâ). Remove repetition for clarity.
âReference to Literatureâ improvement suggests generic reference. Specify targeted databases or key papers to strengthen credibility.
Strengths table articulates key successes, but could note data precision strengths in processed data for balance.
Extension section provides interesting future ideas; consider including statistical methods (e.g., ANOVA) to evaluate varied projectiles.

rubricData.name: Physics IA
rubricData.rubricSpecificInfo: âThe Physics Internal Assessment (IA) is a critical component of the IB Diploma Programme, providing students with the opportunity to design and conduct independent investigations into physics phenomena. This assessment emphasizes the development of practical skills, analytical thinking, and a deep understanding of scientific methodologies. By engaging in the IA, students not only apply theoretical knowledge to real-world scenarios but also cultivate competencies in research design, data analysis, and critical evaluation, all of which are essential for academic and professional success in scientific fields. â
